description.en: Leguminous small tree widely grown on paddy bunds and around home gardens across Sri Lanka's Low and Mid country Wet and Dry regions (hot climates; not successful in cool mountains). Leaves and flowers are both edible curry ingredients. Fixes nitrogen — valued as a green-manure and on-farm fodder. Propagated by seeds, air-layering, or (for the 'Haritha' variety) stem cuttings. DOA recommends 2 × 2 m spacing; leaf harvest starts 8 months after planting, flower harvest at 12 months.

spacing_cm.quote: Plants should be spaced at 2.0 meters between rows and 2.0 meters within rows

duration_days.quote: Leaf harvesting begins approximately 8 months after planting, while flowers become available after one year

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: 4000 – 8000 kgs/ac of leaves can be harvested at a time
